goals: Produce a single-file `parallax` executable per supported platform that requires no runtime dependencies.
Automate the build and publish process so every tagged release produces ready-to-download binaries with zero manual steps.
Keep binary size reasonable by excluding heavy inference deps (torch, transformers, comfy_diffusion) — the installer subcommands pull those at runtime via uv.

userStories.title: Build standalone binary locally
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want to run a single command locally so that I can produce a `parallax` binary for my current platform and test it before tagging a release.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Running `uv run pyinstaller parallax.spec` produces a single-file executable at `dist/parallax` on Linux/macOS or `dist/parallax.exe` on Windows.
The produced binary runs `parallax --help` and `parallax install --help` without any Python installation present on the machine.
The binary does not bundle `torch`, `torchvision`, `torchaudio`, `transformers`, or `comfy_diffusion` — they are listed in `excludes` in the `.spec` file.
The binary size is under 50 MB.

userStories.title: CI builds binaries on every version tag
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want GitHub Actions to automatically build and publish platform binaries whenever I push a `v*.*.*` tag so that users always have up-to-date downloads without manual steps.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A workflow file `.github/workflows/release-cli.yml` triggers on `push` to tags matching `v*.*.*` and on `workflow_dispatch`.
The workflow runs three parallel jobs: `linux` on `ubuntu-latest`, `macos` on `macos-latest` (produces a universal2 binary), and `windows` on `windows-latest`.
Each job: checks out the repo, installs uv, installs only the CLI build deps (`uv sync --group cli-build --no-group dev`), runs PyInstaller with `parallax.spec`, and produces the binary.
The macOS job compiles a universal binary using `--target-arch universal2` so it runs natively on both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
Each job generates a SHA256 checksum file alongside the binary (`parallax-linux-x86_64.sha256`, `parallax-macos-universal.sha256`, `parallax-windows-x86_64.exe.sha256`).

After all three build jobs succeed, a final `release` job uses `softprops/action-gh-release` to create or update the GitHub Release for the tag and uploads all 6 files (3 binaries + 3 checksums) as release assets.
If any build job fails, the release job does not run.

userStories.title: Binary is self-identified
userStories.description: As a user, I want `parallax --version` to print the version matching the GitHub Release tag so that I know exactly which binary I am running.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `parallax --version` prints `parallax 1.x.x` where `1.x.x` matches the `[project].version` field in `pyproject.toml` at the time of the build.
The version is baked into the binary at build time via a build-time constant (not read from an installed package at runtime, since comfy-diffusion is not bundled).

functionalRequirements.description: A `parallax.spec` PyInstaller spec file is added at the repo root. It sets `onefile=True`, `console=True`, `name="parallax"`, and lists `torch`, `torchvision`, `torchaudio`, `transformers`, `comfy`, `comfy_diffusion`, `cv2`, `numpy`, `PIL` in `excludes`.
A `[project.optional-dependencies]` group `cli-build` is added to `pyproject.toml` containing `pyinstaller>=6.0` so the build dep is trackable without polluting the main deps.
The CI `macos` job passes `--target-arch universal2` to PyInstaller and produces a single `parallax-macos-universal` asset (not two separate arm64/x86_64 files).

Release asset names follow the convention `parallax-{platform}[.exe]` exactly: `parallax-linux-x86_64`, `parallax-macos-universal`, `parallax-windows-x86_64.exe` — the bootstrap script (PRD-003) constructs download URLs from these names.
SHA256 checksums are generated with `sha256sum` (Linux/macOS) or `CertUtil` (Windows) and written to `{asset-name}.sha256` as a single line: `<hex>  <filename>`.
The version constant baked into the binary is read from `pyproject.toml` at build time via a `__version__` module generated by the `.spec` file's `Analysis` step.
